1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Multiple Thai government and university sources confirm cool-season preference; Useful Tropical Plants documents the seed-set issue below 500m elevation.

2_climate.rationale: Temperature ranges (15-25C optimal, 4-32C tolerance) and bolting threshold above 24-27C are independently confirmed across PlantVillage, Useful Tropical Plants, and Penn State Extension.

3_soil.rationale: pH 6.2-6.8 framed as a general Apiaceae soil principle (no source-specific attribution); soil texture and organic-matter preparation guidance corroborated by Utah State Extension and OPSMOAC Phichit.

4_water.rationale: Utah State Extension's establishment-then-reduce watering guidance, drought-tolerance noted by Kasetsart KU repository, and Penn State Extension's ground-level (not overhead) watering for leaf-spot prevention all converge.

5_planting.rationale: Direct-sow due to taproot from Wisconsin Extension; seed-soak overnight and 20-30cm row spacing from Phichit OPSMOAC; succession sowing well-documented.

6_care.rationale: Slow-bolt cultivar names ('Calypso', 'Leisure', 'Confetti'), afternoon shade, mulching for soil temperature, and thinning are all Penn State and Wisconsin Extension guidance.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: PlantVillage Penn State is the authoritative pest/disease source covering bacterial leaf spot, damping-off, powdery mildew, aphids, armyworms, and root-knot nematode; Penn State Extension corroborates.

8_harvest.rationale: Leaf 30-45 DAS and seed 90-120 DAS timelines from Phichit OPSMOAC; postharvest cold-chain principles from PHTNet CMU; cut-and-come-again from Penn State Extension.

9_economics.rationale: Yield range 1,300-1,900 kg/rai documented by Phichit OPSMOAC; seasonal price spikes referenced by Kanchanaburi OPSMOAC. Yield framed as recorded range, not guarantee.

10_thailand_notes.rationale: JIRCAS Thai Vegetables Database documents culinary uses; Useful Tropical Plants confirms highland advantage for seed production; Kanchanaburi OPSMOAC notes cool-season preference for Thai growers.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: Foreign-source synthesis cleanly contrasts temperate-season advice (Wisconsin/Penn State/Utah) with tropical applicability (Useful Tropical Plants), explicitly flagging what does and does not transfer to Thailand.

overall_rationale: All 12 sources HTTP-verified by Researcher; Thai government, Thai university, JIRCAS, and four US/international extension sources converge on key claims. Yield section deliberately rated medium because it relies on a single Thai source and is presented as recorded range rather than guarantee.
